nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; lives commensally among branches of Acropora and other branching SPS coral colonies, typically at depths of 2–20 m.

diet.notes: Generally accepts frozen mysis shrimp, enriched brine shrimp, finely chopped table shrimp, and prepared carnivore foods readily. In the wild, has been observed feeding on small invertebrates and zooplankton drawn from the water column over its host coral.

avoidWith: SPS-only Acropora-dominated displays where polyp nipping would be costly

summary: A tiny yellow coral-dwelling goby (5–6.6 cm) that perches on branching SPS corals. Generally peaceful and easy to feed, but produces a defensive skin toxin and is known to nip Acropora polyps.
careNotes: The Citron Clown Goby (Gobiodon citrinus) is a small (~5–6.6 cm), yellow-bodied coral-dwelling goby with blue markings around the eyes and gills. Body coloration is variable, ranging from pale yellow through olive-green to dark brown depending on individual and mood. Like other Gobiodon species, it lives commensally on branched Acropora colonies in the wild and tends to perch on rockwork or coral branches in the aquarium. A tightly-fitted lid is generally advised — although these are less jumpy than benthic gobies, escape from open tanks has been reported. Reef compatibility is best described as 'with caution': individuals have been documented nipping at the polyps and bases of Acropora and possibly other SPS corals, and breeding pairs may lay eggs on coral branches, causing tissue recession. Generally tolerated in mixed reefs with mature, well-stocked SPS colonies, but should be avoided in SPS-only display tanks. Defensive skin toxin: this species (and other Gobiodon) is reported to secrete a crinotoxin (gobiotoxin) when stressed or dying — has been known to kill other small fish in confined quarters, so a careful acclimation and rapid removal of any deceased specimen are advised. Best kept singly or as an established mated pair; individuals may quarrel with conspecifics in smaller tanks. Tank minimum: LiveAquaria lists 10 gallons (~38 L); this file uses 75 L as a more cautious aquarium-hobby standard accounting for water-quality stability and the option of a small mixed-reef community. Sources: LiveAquaria (primary, aquarium husbandry); FishBase (secondary, taxonomy, wild distribution, and max size).
breedingNotes: Has been reared in captivity (FishBase). Oviparous, demersal spawner — pairs lay adhesive eggs on coral branches near their host colony. Males generally tend the eggs. Detailed hobby-scale breeding protocols are not widely documented.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary aquarium-husbandry source. Provided tank size (10 gal), water parameters, temperament, diet, reef-safe rating, and Acropora-nipping caveat.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked scientific name, family (Gobiidae), order (Gobiiformes), max size (6.6 cm TL vs LiveAquaria 2 in / 5 cm — averaged across the range 5–6.6 cm), preferred temperature (24.7–29°C, mean 27.9°C) and broader Indo-West Pacific distribution. FishBase also confirmed the toxic-mucus defense and Acropora-commensal habit.

fish.breedingNotes: Has been reared in captivity but is not common in the hobby; pairs spawn adhesive eggs on coral branches.
